 {"id":"git_safety_guard-99e.8","title":"Optional: token-aware keyword gating (reduce substring false triggers)","description":"## Objective

Improve keyword gating so it is less substring-based and more shell-token aware.

## Background

Current gating uses raw substring search (`memmem::find`) for keywords like `git`, `rm`, `docker`, etc.

This can cause unnecessary pack evaluation on commands like:

- `cat .gitignore` (contains `git`)
- `echo digit` (contains `git`)

While this usually still allows, it:

- adds unnecessary overhead
- increases risk of accidental false positives if any future pattern is less strictly anchored

## Proposed approach

- Reuse the execution-context tokenizer to locate the first word of each command segment.
- Apply pack keyword gating primarily to:
  - segment command name
  - unquoted words in executable contexts

If tokenizer is not yet available, consider a cheap boundary heuristic:

- only treat `git` as present if it appears as a whole word boundary (`\bgit\b`) (but beware performance of regex; prefer manual boundary checks)

## Acceptance criteria

- Demonstrated reduction in unnecessary pack evaluations (microbench).
- No false negatives introduced (must not miss real `git`/`rm` invocations).

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-99e.9","title":"Audit pack keyword gating (avoid false negatives; tune performance)","description":"## Objective

Ensure pack-level keyword gating (`Pack.keywords` + `Pack::might_match`) does not introduce false negatives and provides good performance.

## Background

Each pack uses a keyword list as a quick reject:

- If **no keyword** is found, the pack skips all pattern matching.

This is a performance feature, but it is also a correctness risk:

- Missing keywords can cause false negatives (dangerous commands allowed).

Once we fix global pack reachability, keyword gating becomes even more important.

## Work plan

1. For each built-in pack:
   - review each destructive regex
   - ensure at least one keyword reliably appears in realistic invocations that should match

2. Add unit tests per pack:
   - at least 1 “keyword present → pattern matches” test
   - at least 1 “keyword absent → pack skipped” test (only where correct)

3. Consider whether certain packs should have empty keywords (always check) if patterns are cheap or risks are high.

## Acceptance criteria

- No pack has a destructive pattern that can match a realistic command without containing any of the pack’s keywords.
- Added tests would fail if keywords are removed or patterns are expanded unsafely.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-9eq","title":"E2E: doctor/install/uninstall + config discovery","description":"## Objective

Add E2E coverage for `dcg doctor`, `install`, `uninstall`, and config discovery behavior.

## Scope

- Use isolated temp HOME/XDG_CONFIG_HOME to avoid touching real user files.
- Validate: missing settings/config → doctor reports and `--fix` creates safely.
- Validate: existing settings/config → doctor is idempotent (no overwrite).
- Validate: uninstall removes hook without deleting unrelated settings.

## Logging

- Capture stdout/stderr for each step and include in failure output.

## Acceptance criteria

- E2E script passes locally and in CI.
- No modification to real user HOME.
- Clear failure logs for misconfigurations.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-9np","title":"Add Codecov coverage badge to README","description":"# Task: Add Codecov Coverage Badge to README

## What to Do

Add a coverage badge to README.md that displays the current coverage percentage from Codecov.

## Badge Markdown

Add to the badges section at the top of README.md:

```markdown
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard/branch/master/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard)
```

Or using shields.io (more customizable):

```markdown
[![Coverage](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard?label=coverage)](https://codecov.io/gh/Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard)
```

## Badge Placement

Should go near other status badges (CI status, version, etc.) at the top of the README.

## Why This Matters

1. **Visibility** - Anyone viewing the repo sees coverage at a glance
2. **Accountability** - Public commitment to code quality
3. **Trust signal** - Users see the project maintains high test coverage
4. **Motivation** - Team is motivated to keep the badge green

## Dependencies

This task should be done AFTER:
1. Codecov upload is working (git_safety_guard-ypb)
2. codecov.yml is configured (git_safety_guard-u5w)

Otherwise the badge will show "unknown" or fail to load.

## Testing

1. Verify badge renders correctly in GitHub README preview
2. Click badge to ensure it links to Codecov dashboard
3. Verify coverage percentage shown matches actual coverage

## Files to Modify

- `README.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Coverage badge added to README.md
- [ ] Badge displays correct coverage percentage
- [ ] Badge links to Codecov dashboard
- [ ] Badge renders correctly on GitHub","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:20:49.257869733-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T14:20:49.257869733-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-9np","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ypb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:24:37.97697547-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-9np","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-u5w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:24:38.022069509-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-a21","title":"[PACK] search.elasticsearch - Elasticsearch Pack","description":"# Elasticsearch Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Elasticsearch REST API and CLI operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### Index Operations
- `DELETE /index-name` - Index deletion
- `DELETE /_all` - Delete ALL indices
- `DELETE /*` - Wildcard deletion
- `POST /index/_close` - Close index

### Document Operations  
- `DELETE /index/_doc/id`
- `POST /index/_delete_by_query`

### Cluster Operations
- `PUT /_cluster/settings` (dangerous settings)

### curl/httpie patterns
- `curl -X DELETE` to Elasticsearch endpoints
- `http DELETE` to Elasticsearch endpoints

## Safe Patterns
- `GET` requests
- `_search`, `_cat`, `_cluster/health`
- `_count`, `_mapping`, `_settings`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: search.* (git_safety_guard-nax)
- Keywords: elasticsearch, curl (when targeting ES)
- Note: Need to detect REST API patterns

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Index deletion blocked (single, all, wildcard)
- [ ] Document deletion blocked
- [ ] REST API patterns via curl detected
- [ ] Tests cover common ES operations","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:32:21.798252685-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T18:30:45.896817677-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T18:30:45.896817677-05:00","close_reason":"Elasticsearch pack implemented and registry wired (search tier + pack entry)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-a21","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:32.342061191-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-a21","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:32.381918243-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-a21","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:32.419579583-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-a21","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:32.458321807-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-a21","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:32.496677143-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-a21","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:32.535810403-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-a21","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:09.125310338-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-a21","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:09.17378172-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-a52","title":"Fix bypass: quoted command word lets destructive git/rm evade rules","description":"## Background / Motivation

We discovered a **false-negative bypass** where destructive commands are incorrectly allowed when the *executed command word* is quoted.

Examples that must be denied:
- `"git" reset --hard`
- `"rm" -rf /etc`
- `"/usr/bin/git" reset --hard`
- `echo hi; "rm" -rf /etc`
- `sudo -u root "rm" -rf /etc`

This is especially dangerous because it bypasses the core safety guarantees and can enable real data/work loss.

## Root cause

Current destructive/safe regex patterns match on token sequences like `git\s+reset\s+--hard` and `rm\s+-rf ...`.

When the command word is quoted (e.g., `"git"`), the stream contains `git"` / `"git"` and the expected whitespace boundary no longer exists at the command word boundary, so the patterns do not match.

Existing normalization handled only commands starting with `/.../git` or `/.../rm` and did **not** dequote executed command tokens.

## Fix

Centralize the fix in normalization (not in per-pattern regex loosening) to avoid creating new false positives in data contexts.

Implement a conservative normalization pass that:
- tokenizes the command into word/separator tokens,
- finds the **segment command word** (first executed word after wrappers/env-assignments),
- removes *outer quotes* from that command-word token only when it is a single token (no whitespace / separators inside),
- preserves quoted arguments to avoid synthesizing dangerous substrings in data contexts.

Wrapper awareness (for locating the executed command word) includes:
- `sudo` with option + option-arg skipping (e.g., `-u root`)
- `env` options and env assignments
- `command -v/-V` recognized as query mode (not execution)

Separators treated as segment boundaries include `;`, `|`, `||`, `&`, `&&`, `(`, `)`.

## Tests

Add regression coverage:
- Unit tests for `normalize_command()`:
  - dequote executed command words for git/rm and quoted absolute paths
  - dequote after separators and wrappers/options
  - negative tests: do not dequote quoted arguments (e.g., `echo "rm" ...`)
  - negative tests: do not dequote for `command -v` query mode
- E2E tests in `scripts/e2e_test.sh`:
  - ensure the quoted forms above are **BLOCKED**

## Acceptance

- Previously-allowed bypass commands are now denied.
- No new false positives in common data contexts.
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo build --release`, `./scripts/e2e_test.sh` all pass.
","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-08T04:58:32.779202524-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T04:58:44.349806876-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T04:58:44.349806876-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented + added unit + e2e regression coverage","labels":["core","normalization","security"]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.3","title":"CI: scan --git-diff integration tests","description":"## Objective

Add integration tests ensuring `dcg scan --git-diff`:

- returns deterministic results
- handles empty diffs
- handles renamed/moved files gracefully

## Logging requirements

- Tests should log the diff range and files considered.

## Test plan

- Create a temp repo with two commits.
- Modify files across commits to cover:
  - added file
  - modified file
  - renamed file
  - deleted file (should be skipped gracefully)
- Run `dcg scan --git-diff <base>..<head>` and assert:
  - deterministic ordering
  - stable JSON (if using `--json`)
  - correct exit code per `--fail-on`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `dcg scan --git-diff <range>` succeeds on:
  - empty diffs (exit 0, no findings)
  - renames/moves (scans new path, no crash)
  - deletes (skips missing paths, no crash)
- Output is deterministic across runs.
- Tests emit enough logs (diff range, candidate files, filtered files) to debug failures in CI.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive regression
Include a negative fixture in the diff-range test:

- a file where a dangerous string appears only in docs/comments and must not produce a finding

This prevents CI adoption from being derailed by noisy PR checks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:57.803468576-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T23:40:31.751720567-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T23:40:31.751720567-05:00","close_reason":"Added integration tests for dcg scan --git-diff: empty diffs, renamed files, deleted files (skipped), deterministic ordering, mixed operations","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:19:17.551755437-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:16.363785468-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:21.594148009-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:26.844970733-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.4","title":"Optional: package dcg scan as a reusable GitHub Action","description":"## Objective

Package `dcg scan` into a reusable GitHub Action for easier org-scale adoption.

## Why optional

A workflow snippet is enough to start. A marketplace action is accretive once:

- the scan JSON schema is stable
- allowlisting workflow is mature
- false positives are under control

## Requirements

- Action inputs:
  - `fail_on`
  - `scan_paths` or diff mode
  - `comment_on_pr`
- Action outputs:
  - summary counts
  - artifact upload

## Acceptance criteria

- Example repo can adopt by adding a few lines.
- Action pins a specific dcg version (no surprise upgrades).
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### Tests
- Validate the action packaging with an end-to-end workflow in a fixture repo:
  - ensures correct exit codes
  - ensures deterministic output
  - ensures redaction/truncation does not break parsing","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:28:09.562467338-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T14:18:04.781633711-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T14:18:04.781633711-05:00","close_reason":"Created reusable GitHub Action in action/. Includes action.yml with all inputs/outputs, README with examples, binary download with cargo fallback, PR comment support, and version pinning.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:43:33.082509684-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:43:54.301284345-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:43:59.341416862-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:44:04.378980832-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:44:09.420139301-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:44:14.458422044-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:44:19.495891555-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.6","title":"Additional scan extractors (package.json, compose, Terraform, GitLab CI)","description":"## Objective

Expand `dcg scan` coverage with additional *high-signal executable contexts* that frequently contain destructive commands in real repos, while keeping false positives low.

This feature is intentionally **post-MVP**: we only do it after the scan schema + core CLI are stable.

## Why this matters

Teams commonly introduce destructive commands via:

- `package.json` lifecycle scripts
- `docker-compose.yml` `command`/`entrypoint`/`healthcheck.test`
- Terraform provisioners (`local-exec` / `remote-exec`)
- GitLab CI `.gitlab-ci.yml` `script:` arrays

These are *executed contexts*, so scanning them has a high signal-to-noise ratio if extraction is done precisely.

## Guardrails (false-positive immunity)

- Only extract fields that are unquestionably executed.
- Explicitly ignore data-only fields like `description`, `env`, `variables`, `labels`, etc.
- Preserve line numbers and a stable `extractor_id`.
- Provide redaction/truncation controls for potentially sensitive commands.

## Acceptance criteria

- Each extractor has fixtures that prove boundaries (executed extracted; data ignored).
- Extractors handle common YAML/JSON features (arrays, block scalars, anchors/aliases where applicable).
- Output is deterministic and stable for golden tests.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-scan.1` (schema)
- `git_safety_guard-scan.2` (CLI + file selection)

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-skt","title":"[CATEGORY] storage.* - Object Storage Pack Suite","description":"# Object Storage Pack Suite

## Overview
Object storage holds unstructured data (files, media, backups). Bucket deletion or object removal can cause significant data loss. Lifecycle policies can auto-delete.

## Packs to Implement

### storage.s3
- **Why Critical**: S3 is the dominant object storage. Bucket deletion and object removal are common mistakes.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `aws s3 rb` - Remove bucket
  - `aws s3 rb --force` - Force remove with contents
  - `aws s3 rm` with recursive flag - Object deletion
  - `aws s3api delete-bucket`
  - `aws s3api delete-object`
  - Lifecycle policy with expiration
- **Safe Patterns**: `ls`, `cp` (read), `sync` (without --delete), `presign`

### storage.gcs
- **Why Critical**: Google Cloud Storage for GCP workloads.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `gsutil rb` - Remove bucket
  - `gsutil` remove with recursive flag
  - `gcloud storage` remove operations
  - `gcloud storage buckets delete`
- **Safe Patterns**: `ls`, `cat`, `stat`, `du`

### storage.azure_blob
- **Why Critical**: Azure Blob Storage for Azure workloads.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `az storage container delete`
  - `az storage blob delete`
  - `az storage blob delete-batch`
  - `azcopy remove`
- **Safe Patterns**: `list`, `show`, `download`

### storage.minio
- **Why Critical**: MinIO is popular for self-hosted S3-compatible storage.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `mc rb` - Remove bucket
  - `mc` remove with recursive/force flags
- **Safe Patterns**: `ls`, `cat`, `stat`, `diff`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 3 Epic (git_safety_guard-9ic)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Major cloud storage providers covered
- [ ] Recursive deletion patterns detected
- [ ] Force flags recognized
- [ ] Lifecycle policy operations flagged
- [ ] Tests cover common storage operations","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:30:20.654308131-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T14:30:20.654308131-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-skt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-3a1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:35:20.638107812-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-skt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-wy72","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:13:12.708953765-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-skt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-uag6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:13:12.742910359-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-skt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-rkg9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:13:12.778248899-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-skt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.774431182-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-skt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.821544827-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-skt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.869159404-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-skt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.917016148-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-skt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:48.000978133-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-skt","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:48.051864537-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-t1rx","title":"Implement Tier 1 regex heredoc detector with RegexSet","description":"# Tier 1: Fast Regex Heredoc Detection

## Objective

Implement the fast-path heredoc detector using Rust's `regex` crate with RegexSet for multi-pattern matching.

## Why RegexSet?

The `regex` crate's RegexSet allows matching against multiple patterns simultaneously with:
- Aho-Corasick algorithm for literal prefixes
- SIMD acceleration (AVX2, SSE4.2)
- Single pass through input
- O(n) guaranteed time complexity

## Patterns to Implement

```rust
static HEREDOC_PATTERNS: LazyLock<RegexSet> = LazyLock::new(|| {
    RegexSet::new([
        // Bash heredocs
        r"<<-?\s*['\"]?\w+['\"]?",   // <<EOF, <<'EOF', <<-EOF
        r"<<<",                          // Here-strings

        // Inline script flags
        r"\b(python3?|python)\s+-[ce]\s",
        r"\b(ruby|irb)\s+-e\s",
        r"\bperl\s+-[eE]\s",
        r"\bnode\s+-[ep]\s",
        r"\b(sh|bash|zsh|fish)\s+-c\s",

        // Pipe to interpreter
        r"\|\s*(python3?|ruby|perl|node|sh|bash)\b",
        r"\|\s*xargs\s",
    ]).expect("heredoc patterns compile")
});
```

## API

```rust
/// Check if command might contain heredoc/inline script
/// Returns true if ANY pattern matches (fast path triggers Tier 2)
pub fn might_contain_heredoc(cmd: &str) -> bool;

/// Get which patterns matched (for debugging/logging)
pub fn matched_heredoc_patterns(cmd: &str) -> Vec<usize>;
```

## Performance Target

- < 10 microseconds for typical commands
- Zero allocations on non-match path
- Must have ZERO false negatives (can have false positives)

## Test Cases

### Must Match (true positives)
- `python3 << 'EOF'\nprint('hi')\nEOF`
- `bash -c 'echo hello'`
- `node -e "console.log(1)"`
- `curl http://x.com | bash`
- `cat << EOF | python3`

### Must NOT Match (true negatives)
- `git status`
- `ls -la`
- `cargo build`
- `echo "hello world"`

### May Match (acceptable false positives)
- `grep -e pattern file` (has -e but not dangerous)
- `echo "<<EOF"` (string contains heredoc marker)

## Dependencies

- regex = "1.10" (add to Cargo.toml)
- Depends on: Two-tier architecture design (2ta)

## Acceptance Criteria

- `might_contain_heredoc` returns true for all “Must Match” cases.
- `might_contain_heredoc` returns false for all “Must NOT Match” cases.
- Detector adds effectively-zero overhead for non-matching commands (validate via benchmark once perf harness exists).
- Patterns are compiled once (static) and never per-invocation.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyMill","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:00:00-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T01:43:47.858027873-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T01:43:47.858027873-05:00","close_reason":"Implementation complete in src/heredoc.rs. All acceptance criteria met:
- check_triggers() (equiv. to might_contain_heredoc) detects all heredoc/inline script patterns
- matched_triggers() provides debugging indices
- Uses LazyLock<RegexSet> for static compilation, SIMD-accelerated matching
- 12 unit tests passing covering all required test cases
- Additional patterns included: fish -c, xargs pipe, <<~ Ruby heredocs","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-t1rx","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-2ta","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:28:32.034477208-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-t1rx","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:50:39.537459716-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-t2r","title":"[PACK] dns.generic - Generic DNS Tools Pack","description":"# Generic DNS Tools Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for generic DNS tools (nsupdate, dig, host, nslookup).

## Destructive Patterns
### nsupdate (Dynamic DNS updates)
- `nsupdate` with `delete` commands in input
- `nsupdate -l` (local update)
- Piping delete commands to nsupdate

### Zone Transfer Risks
- `dig axfr` to untrusted servers (data exfiltration)
- Zone file manipulation

## Safe Patterns
- `dig` (query only)
- `host` (lookup only)
- `nslookup` (query only)
- `dig +short`, `dig +trace`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: dns.* (git_safety_guard-x3g)
- Keywords: nsupdate, dig, host, nslookup
- Note: dig/host/nslookup are read-only, nsupdate is dangerous

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for nsupdate delete patterns
- [ ] Unit tests for safe lookup commands
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/dns_generic.txt`
- [ ] Logging: trace nsupdate command parsing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] nsupdate delete commands blocked
- [ ] Safe DNS lookups allowed
- [ ] Zone transfer warnings (optional)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:10:25.826860714-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:47:57.886574331-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:47:57.886574331-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-t2r","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:36.845844809-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-t2r","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:36.888648214-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-t2r","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:36.932442384-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-t2r","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:36.974907643-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-t2r","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:37.017371299-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-t2r","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:37.05868985-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-t2r","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:10.809612175-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-t2r","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:10.857009924-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-t8x","title":"Epic: False Positive Immunity (Execution Context)","description":"# Epic: False Positive Immunity (Execution Context)

## Why this epic exists

A destructive-command guard must be *trusted*. Repeated false positives destroy velocity and lead users to disable the guard entirely.

The core failure mode is **context blindness**: matching dangerous substrings in places where they are clearly *data*, not *executed code*.

Examples that must be allowed:

- `bd create --description="This pattern blocks rm -rf"`
- `git commit -m "Fix git reset --hard detection"`
- `echo "example: git push --force"`
- `rg -n "rm -rf" src/main.rs`

Currently, a purely regex-based guard will often match `rm -rf` (or similar) inside quoted strings and block legitimate documentation/testing work.

## Key idea

Introduce an **Execution Context layer** that classifies which parts of the command line are actually executed vs merely data.

Then, apply destructive patterns only to executable contexts:

- Executed command words and arguments
- Pipe targets (right side of `|`)
- Command substitution (`$(...)`, backticks)
- Inline interpreter strings (`bash -c`, `python -c`, `node -e`, etc.)
- Heredoc bodies (handled by the heredoc epic)

…and **skip** matching for clearly data-only contexts:

- Option values for known “documentation/metadata” commands (Beads `bd`, git commit messages, etc.)
- Grep/Ripgrep pattern arguments
- Echo/printf arguments

## Goals

1. **Dramatically reduce false positives** for doc/test workflows while preserving true-positive blocks for real execution.
2. **Keep performance high:** the non-risky path should stay microseconds.
3. **Provide explainability hooks:** when a command is allowed due to data-context detection, we should be able to explain why (for debugging).

## Architectural constraints

- This layer runs before pack regex evaluation and before AST/heredoc scanning.
- It must be conservative: if context is ambiguous, treat as executable (or escalate to deeper analysis), but avoid blocking obvious documentation strings.

## Success Criteria

- The examples in “Why this epic exists” are allowed.
- Real executions are still blocked:
  - `bash -c "rm -rf /"`
  - `echo hi | bash -c "rm -rf /"`
  - `python -c "import os; os.system('rm -rf /')"`
- Context classification has regression tests for:
  - quoting (single/double/escaped)
  - pipes
  - command substitution
  - xargs
  - interpreter flags (-c/-e/-p)

## Notes for future self

- If we add a “warn mode” or confidence scoring, this epic provides the context signals.
- Don’t overfit to specific tools; start with a minimal safe-string-arg registry and expand based on real false positives.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:57:23.162019558-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T14:12:48.458567358-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T14:12:48.458567358-05:00","close_reason":"All required subtasks complete: 2cu (design), fpim (implementation), t8x.1 (registry), t8x.2 (context classification), t8x.3 (E2E regression), t8x.4 (safe cleanup). Only t8x.5 (P4 optional confidence scoring) remains.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-t8x","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:53:37.084188042-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
